paras.text: College ke notice board pe ek naya notice chipka tha, itni ajeeb formal language me likha tha ki {{A}} aur {{B}} dono ruk gaye padhne ke liye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (zor se padhte hue, mazak udate hue) 'It is imperative that every student BE present in the auditorium by 9 AM sharp.' Ye 'BE' kya hai yahan? 'is' kyu nahi likha?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Arre ye galti nahi hai, ye ek KHAAS grammar form hai — Present Subjunctive kehte hain isko! Bahut official/formal notices, rules, aur demands me use hota hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Present Subjunctive? Naam se hi darr lag raha hai. Samjhao na, kya scene hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul simple hai, dekhte hi dekhte samajh aa jayega! Jab kisi cheez ko bahut ZAROORI, DEMAND, ya FORMAL SUGGESTION ki tarah bola jaata hai, tab uske 'that' clause ke andar wala verb hamesha BASE FORM me rehta hai — chahe subject 'he/she/it' ho, chahe sentence present ho ya past, koi fark nahi padta.It is imperative that every student be present in the auditorium by 9 AM Dekho — 'every student' third-person singular hai, normal grammar me 'is' aata, but yahan 'BE' hi rehta hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hairan) Ye to bilkul ulta lagta hai jo humne pehle seekha tha! To ye sirf notice-board wali cheez hai, ya roz ki English me bhi hota hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Roz ki spoken English me kam hota hai, but FORMAL writing — notices, rules, official letters, meeting minutes, exam answers — inme bahut common hai. Aur SSC me to ye favorite error-spotting topic hai, kyunki 90% log 'is/was/are' likh dete hain galti se.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ek cheez samajh nahi aayi — 'It is necessary that everyone DOES his work efficiently' — ye galat kyu hai? Sunne me to sahi lagta hai, 'everyone' ke saath 'does' hi to aata hai normally!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bahut hi important sawaal, isi se poora subjunctive samajh aata hai! Dhyan se socho — 'everyone DOES his work efficiently' ka matlab kya hoga agar ye sach me sahi hota? Iska matlab hoga — ye ek FACT hai, ki sabhi log ALREADY apna kaam efficiently karte hain, ye unka roz ka habit/reality hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue) Haan, matlab wahi jo ho raha hai already.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul! Ab socho, agar sach me sab efficiently kaam kar hi rahe hain, to 'It is necessary that...' bolne ki zarurat hi kya hai? Jo pehle se ho raha hai, uske liye 'zaroori hai' bolna hi ajeeb hai! Yehi to point hai — 'It is necessary' ka matlab hai ye abhi NAHI ho raha, ya pakka nahi hai ki hoga, isliye hum ye DEMAND/REQUIREMENT bata rahe hain ki ye HONA CHAHIYE — jo abhi reality nahi hai, ek expectation hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (aankhein badi karte hue) Ohhh! Ab samajh aaya — 'DOES' se lagta hai ye already sach hai, 'FACT' hai. But hum to REQUIREMENT bata rahe hain, kuch jo HONA CHAHIYE, abhi guaranteed nahi hai. Isliye 'does' galat hai, 'DO' (base form) sahi hai, kyunki wo requirement/wish dikhata hai, fact nahi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Ekdum perfect samjhe tumne! Yehi hai subjunctive ka asli logic — BASE FORM use karke hum batate hain 'ye abhi sach nahi hai, but ye HONA CHAHIYE/ZAROORI hai.' Agar hum 'is/does/are' jaisa NORMAL (Indicative Mood) form use karte, to matlab hota 'ye already sach hai' — jo sense hi nahi banata jab tum 'necessary/essential/demand' jaisa word use kar rahe ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, chalo poora samjhao — kaunse words ke baad ye BASE FORM wala rule lagta hai? Poori list chahiye mujhe.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, do groups me yaad rakho. Pehla group — ADJECTIVES, jo 'It is + adjective + that' pattern me aate hain: essential, necessary, important, imperative, vital, crucial, desirable, urgent, obligatory. Doosra group — VERBS jo seedhe use hote hain: suggest, recommend, demand, insist, propose, require, urge, request, order, desire, prefer. In sabke baad, wahi FACT-vs-REQUIREMENT wala logic hamesha kaam karega — jo bhi word ho, matlab yehi hai ki 'ye abhi sach nahi, hona chahiye'.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook nikaal ke) Itni lambi list! Chalo ek-ek karke example do, but har baar dono versions dikhana — agar galti se present/normal form laga diya to kya WRONG meaning banega, aur base form se kya SAHI meaning banta hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Perfect approach, chalo isi tarah karte hain, isi notice board se shuru karte hain. Agla notice dekho — 'It is essential that all bikes be parked outside the gate.' Ab socho, agar koi galti se likh de 'all bikes ARE parked outside the gate' — iska kya matlab hoga?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue) 'are parked' ka matlab hoga — sab bikes ALREADY bahar khadi hain, ye ek current fact hai. To phir 'it is essential' bolne ka koi matlab nahi rahega, kyunki jo maang rahe ho wo to already ho hi raha hai!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Ekdum sahi! Isiliye 'be parked' (base form) sahi hai — iska matlab hai, abhi ye guaranteed nahi hai ki sab bikes bahar rakhi jaayengi, isliye ek RULE/REQUIREMENT banaya ja raha hai ki aisa HONA CHAHIYE. Agar sab already follow kar rahe hote, to notice lagane ki zarurat hi nahi padti!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (khush hoke) Ab bilkul clear hai — jahan bhi 'it is essential/necessary' dikhe, wahan agar 's' wala normal form use kiya, to matlab hoga 'ye already ho raha hai', jo point hi khatam kar deta hai us sentence ka. Ek aur example do isi tarah, 'imperative' wale ka.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — hostel warden ka notice: 'It is imperative that every student RETURN before 9 PM.' Agar koi likhe 'every student RETURNS before 9 PM' — iska matlab kya hoga?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'Returns' ka matlab — sab students ALREADY 9 baje se pehle aa jaate hain, ye unki habit/fact hai. To warden ko notice lagane ki zarurat hi nahi thi agar sab pehle se hi time pe aa rahe hain!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul sahi! Isiliye 'RETURN' (base form) — warden keh rahi hai 'ye HONA CHAHIYE', kyunki abhi sab time pe nahi aa rahe (tabhi to strict notice lagi hai). Agar sabhi already follow kar rahe hote, koi notice ki zarurat hi nahi hoti.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Ab samajh aa raha hai warden itni gussa kyu rehti hai — kyunki 'imperative' tabhi bolti hai jab log follow NAHI kar rahe! Ek aur do, 'vital' wala.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — cricket coach: 'It is vital that every player ARRIVE at the ground by 6 AM.' Agar likhe 'every player ARRIVES at the ground by 6 AM' — matlab?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'Arrives' matlab — sab players ALREADY time pe pahunch jaate hain, fact hai. To coach ko itna zor dene ki zarurat hi nahi hoti agar sab pehle se punctual hain!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! 'ARRIVE' (base form) se pata chalta hai — abhi sab time pe nahi aa rahe (isiliye coach itna zor de raha hai), aur ye HONA CHAHIYE agli baar se. Dekha, yehi pattern har jagah kaam karta hai — jaise hi tum 'agar normal form use karein to kya matlab banega' poochhte ho, turant clear ho jaata hai base form kyu zaroori hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Ye trick bahut achhi hai — har trigger word ke sath ye sawaal poochna: 'agar normal/indicative form use karein, to kya iska matlab hoga ki ye already ho raha hai? Agar haan, to wo GALAT hai, kyunki 'necessary/essential/imperative' jaisa word hi keh raha hai ye abhi NAHI ho raha.' Ab VERBS wala group bhi isi tarah karo — 'suggest' aur 'recommend' se shuru karte hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — 'I suggest that my son EAT more vegetables.' Agar likhe 'my son EATS more vegetables' — kya matlab hoga?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'Eats' matlab — beta already zyada vegetables khaata hai, fact hai. To 'I suggest' bolne ka koi matlab nahi, kyunki jo suggest kar rahe ho wo to already ho raha hai!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! 'EAT' (base form) sahi hai — matlab abhi nahi kha raha, aur maa/doctor chahte hain ye HO. Same logic 'recommend' me bhi — 'I recommend that he EXERCISE daily.' Agar 'EXERCISES' likhoge, to matlab hoga wo already roz exercise karta hai, jo recommend karne ki zarurat khatam kar deta hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (pakka samajh ke) Achha, ab 'demand' aur 'insist' bhi karo isi tarah — ye to bahut strong words hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — angry customer: 'I demand that the manager REFUND my money.' Agar 'REFUNDS' likha, matlab hoga manager already refund kar raha hai — to customer ko 'demand' karne ki zarurat hi nahi! 'REFUND' (base form) sahi hai kyunki abhi refund NAHI hua hai, customer demand kar raha hai ki ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Haha, sahi hai, agar manager already paisa wapas kar raha hota, customer chillata hi kyu! Ab 'insist' bhi karo isi tarah.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — 'The teacher insisted that he BE more careful.' Agar 'IS more careful' likha, matlab wo already careful hai — to teacher ko insist karne ki zarurat nahi hoti! 'BE' (base form) sahi hai, kyunki abhi careful nahi hai, teacher chahti hai wo careful HO.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (pakka likhte hue) Ye pattern to ab dimag me bilkul baith gaya — kisi bhi trigger word ke baad, agar normal 's/is/was' wala form use karo, to matlab ban jaata hai 'ye already sach hai', jo point hi kaat deta hai us sentence ka. Base form batata hai 'ye abhi sach nahi, hona chahiye'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi samajh gaye! Ab do aakhri examples, roz ki zindagi se — 'require' aur wo casual 'wants that' wala. 'The exam rules require that every student SIGN the attendance sheet.' Agar 'SIGNS' likha, matlab har student already sign karta hai — to rule banane ki zarurat hi nahi!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Aur 'wants that' wala bhi isi tarah — 'The boss wants that everybody COME on time.' Agar 'COMES' likha, matlab sab already time pe aate hain — to boss ko 'want' karne ki zarurat nahi padegi, wo pehle se ho raha hoga!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect, bilkul khud samajh gaye pattern! Ab ek zaroori cheez — chahe reporting verb PAST me ho ('suggested', 'demanded', 'insisted'), that-clause ka verb PHIR BHI base form hi rahega, kabhi past nahi hota. Kyunki wahi FACT-vs-REQUIREMENT logic tab bhi kaam karta hai — chahe demand kal ki thi ya aaj, requirement to abhi bhi 'nahi hui hai, honi chahiye' wali hi hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (dhyan se) Ohh, matlab time badalne se bhi ye logic nahi badalta — requirement/wish hamesha 'abhi tak poora nahi hua' wali baat hoti hai, isliye base form hamesha same rehta hai chahe reporting verb kisi bhi tense me ho.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Ab ek naya, related topic — jab that-clause PASSIVE VOICE me ho, tab kya hota hai?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Passive matlab jaise 'kaam kiya jaata hai' wala? Samjhao.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: It is necessary that the work be done efficiently — dekho, yahan bhi wahi logic hai. Agar likha 'the work IS DONE efficiently', matlab hoga kaam already efficiently ho raha hai, fact hai — to 'necessary' bolne ka matlab hi nahi. 'BE DONE' (base form) sahi hai, matlab abhi efficiently nahi ho raha, hona chahiye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me dono side likhte hue) Ohh, matlab passive me bhi wahi FACT-vs-REQUIREMENT wala hi test lagega — 'is done' ka matlab fact, 'be done' ka matlab requirement. Ek aur passive example do.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — 'The workers demanded that their salary BE INCREASED.' Agar 'IS INCREASED' likha, matlab salary already badh rahi hai — to demand karne ki zarurat nahi! 'BE INCREASED' sahi hai, kyunki abhi nahi badhi, workers chahte hain badhe.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Perfect, ab poora logic — active ho ya passive, hamesha wahi test — 'agar normal form likhein, to kya matlab banega ki ye already sach hai? Agar haan aur sentence me necessary/demand/suggest jaisa word hai, to wo GALAT hai, base form hi sahi hai.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (khush hoke) Bilkul perfect summary! Chalo ab isi logic se practice karte hain — main tumhe sentence dungi WITH BOTH VERSIONS, tum bataoge kaunsa sahi hai aur KYU.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Chalo, ready hoon!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Pehla — 'It is imperative that every student ARRIVES before the bell.' ya 'ARRIVE before the bell.' Kaunsa sahi, aur kyu?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'ARRIVE' sahi hai — kyunki 'ARRIVES' ka matlab hota sab already time pe aate hain (fact), jo 'imperative' bolne ka point khatam kar deta. 'ARRIVE' batata hai ye abhi nahi ho raha, hona chahiye.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! Doosra — 'The coach insisted that every player PRACTICES for two hours.' ya 'PRACTICE for two hours.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'PRACTICE' sahi — 'PRACTICES' ka matlab hota sab already do ghante practice karte hain, to insist karne ki zarurat nahi hoti. 'PRACTICE' (base form) sahi hai kyunki requirement hai, fact nahi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Perfect! Teesra, passive wala — 'It is necessary that the report IS SUBMITTED by Friday.' ya 'BE SUBMITTED by Friday.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'BE SUBMITTED' sahi — 'IS SUBMITTED' ka matlab hoga report already time pe submit ho rahi hai (fact), jo 'necessary' bolne ka point kaat deta. 'BE SUBMITTED' requirement dikhata hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (zor se taali maarte hue) Full marks, bilkul perfect logic har baar! Ab tum sach me samajh ke seekhe ho, sirf ratt ke nahi — ye sabse achhi baat hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Sach me, ab wo notice board bhi mujhe alag lagega — har baar 'be/come/attend' dekhoonga to samajh jaunga ye ek unfulfilled requirement hai, fact nahi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Bas ek promise karo — 'It is essential that you revise this topic before the exam!' — dekha, main bhi khud subjunctive use kar rahi hoon, aur ab tumhe pata bhi hai ki iska matlab hai — abhi tumne revise nahi kiya, karna chahiye!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (salute karte hue, hasm ke) Yes madam, samajh gaya — 'It is obligatory that I REVISE' (abhi nahi kiya, karna hai), na ki 'I REVISE' (jaisे already kar raha hoon)!

quiz.q: Explain in one line: why is 'It is necessary that everyone does his work efficiently' WRONG?
quiz.answer: 'Does' (Indicative) states this is already happening as a fact/habit — but 'It is necessary' means it's NOT guaranteed yet, it's a requirement. The correct form is base form 'do', which expresses a requirement, not a fact.
quiz.explain: Subjunctive base form signals 'this should happen, not that it already does' — using the normal 's' form contradicts the very meaning of 'necessary/essential/demand'.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'It is imperative that everybody comes on time.'
quiz.answer: It is imperative that everybody come on time.
quiz.explain: If 'comes' (fact form) were correct, it would mean everybody already arrives on time — making the notice pointless. 'Come' (base form) correctly signals this is a requirement, not yet a fact.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The boss wants that everybody comes on time.'
quiz.answer: The boss wants that everybody come on time.
quiz.explain: Same logic — 'comes' would mean people already arrive on time (no need to want it); 'come' (base form) correctly shows this is a requirement.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The rule requires that every member signs the form.'
quiz.answer: The rule requires that every member sign the form.
quiz.explain: 'Signs' would mean members already sign it as routine, making the rule pointless. 'Sign' (base form) correctly shows this is being required, not already true.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The teacher suggested that he was more careful in future.'
quiz.answer: The teacher suggested that he be more careful in future.
quiz.explain: Even though 'suggested' is past tense, the requirement itself is still 'not yet true, should become true' — so the base form 'be' stays the same regardless of the reporting verb's tense.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'It is necessary that the work is done efficiently.'
quiz.answer: It is necessary that the work be done efficiently.
quiz.explain: 'Is done' would mean the work is already being done efficiently (a fact), which contradicts 'necessary'. Passive subjunctive uses 'be + V3' ('be done') to show it's a requirement.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The workers demanded that their salary is increased.'
quiz.answer: The workers demanded that their salary be increased.
quiz.explain: 'Is increased' would mean the salary is already being increased (fact) — then there'd be nothing to demand. 'Be increased' (base form) correctly shows this is the requirement being demanded.

quiz.q: Fill in the blank and explain: 'It is vital that she ___ (attend) the interview.' What would 'attends' incorrectly imply?
quiz.answer: attend (base form). 'Attends' would incorrectly imply she already/habitually attends interviews as a fact, making the word 'vital' pointless.
quiz.explain: The base form correctly expresses that her attending is a requirement/necessity that hasn't happened yet, not an existing fact.
